text: A researcher asks an LLM to suggest possible titles for a paper.
A researcher asks an LLM to suggest a structure for a research paper.
A researcher asks an LLM to generate keywords for their paper.
A researcher asks an LLM to shorten a proposal to meet a page limit, then checks the result carefully.
A researcher asks an LLM to rewrite sentences in a paper for clarity, then reviews the changes.
A non-native-English researcher asks an LLM to improve grammar and language in a paper, then reviews the result.
A researcher asks an LLM to suggest conference speakers on a topic.
A researcher asks an LLM to suggest related work they may have missed.
A researcher runs an LLM agent producing daily arXiv summaries to decide what papers to read.
A student asks an LLM to explain a mathematical concept.
A researcher asks an LLM to summarize a long paper before deciding whether to read it fully.
A researcher asks an LLM to suggest research questions for a new project.
A student asks an LLM to suggest possible thesis topics.
A researcher asks an LLM to generate code for a data analysis step, then checks the code carefully.
A researcher uses an LLM to extract structured data from a text file, then verifies the results.
A researcher asks an LLM to suggest keywords describing their research paper.
A professor asks an LLM to help design a lecture explaining a difficult concept.
A researcher asks an LLM to summarize referee reports before writing the response to reviewers.
A reviewer asks an LLM to suggest points to consider when reviewing a paper, without uploading the manuscript.
A researcher asks an LLM to generate a first draft outline for a grant proposal.
A researcher asks an LLM to write the introduction of a paper, then carefully revises it.
A researcher asks an LLM to write an abstract based on the paper text, then checks it before submission.
A researcher asks an LLM to write the discussion section from results and notes, then revises it.
A researcher trains an LLM on their own papers to generate first drafts of new manuscripts, which they edit carefully.
A researcher uses an LLM as their main tool for finding relevant papers in an unfamiliar field.
A professor asks an LLM to generate a project plan for a student, then revises it.
An instructor asks an LLM to generate exercise questions, then checks them carefully.
An instructor uses an LLM to grade assignments, then reviews the grades before releasing them.
A researcher asks an LLM to summarize a colleague’s research before meeting them.
A researcher uses two LLM agents debating the feasibility of a project as a brainstorming tool.
A researcher asks an LLM to write the introduction of a paper and submits it with minimal editing.
A researcher asks an LLM to write an abstract and submits it without checking carefully.
A researcher asks an LLM to write large parts of a paper, then lists only human authors.
A researcher asks an LLM to rewrite text from another paper to avoid similarity detection.
A researcher asks an LLM to generate references for a paper and adds them without verifying them.
A student asks an LLM to write a literature review and submits it without verifying citations.
An instructor uses an LLM to generate exam questions and uses them without checking.
An instructor uses an LLM to grade assignments and releases the grades without checking.
A researcher asks a public LLM to help debug code by pasting unpublished research data.
A researcher asks a public LLM to suggest improvements to an unpublished manuscript by pasting sections of it.
A reviewer uploads a confidential manuscript under peer review to a public LLM to draft a referee report.
A reviewer asks an LLM to write a referee report and submits it mostly unchanged.
A committee member uploads confidential grant proposals to a public LLM to summarize them.
A committee member uploads confidential recommendation letters to a public LLM to summarize them.
A researcher uploads proprietary telescope data to a public LLM to analyze it.
A researcher asks an LLM to generate missing data values and reports them as measurements.
A researcher asks an LLM to write a full review article and posts it to arXiv with minimal checking.
A committee uses an LLM to rank grant proposals by quality and novelty and uses the ranking directly.
A researcher uses LLM-generated analysis code in a paper without understanding or verifying it.
A researcher uses AI extensively while writing a paper but does not disclose the AI use despite journal policy requiring it.
A researcher uses an LLM to help write parts of a paper and explicitly acknowledges the AI use because the journal requires disclosure.
A researcher uses an LLM to help write parts of a paper but does not disclose the AI use even though the journal requires it.
A researcher uses an LLM to improve language and clarity in a paper but does not disclose it because they consider it similar to grammar-checking software.
